subject: math
topic_title: Deductive Proof — Triangles, Parallel Lines and Congruency
source_title: New General Mathematics SSS 1 by M.F. Macrae et al
learning_objectives: Prove that the sum of angles in a triangle is 180°
Apply properties of parallel lines cut by a transversal
State and apply properties of parallelograms and rhombuses
Identify congruent triangles and state conditions for congruency

sections.heading: Proof: Angle Sum of a Triangle = 180°
sections.content: Given triangle ABC, produce BC to X and draw CP parallel to BA. Let angle BAC = a₁, angle ABC = b₁, angle BCA = c. Then: a₁ = angle ACE (alternate angles, BA ∥ CP); b₁ = angle ECD (corresponding angles, BA ∥ CP). Since c + angle ACE + angle ECD = 180° (angles on a straight line BX), we have c + a₁ + b₁ = 180°, i.e., A + B + C = 180°.

sections.heading: Angles on Parallel Lines Cut by a Transversal
sections.content: When a transversal cuts two parallel lines, it creates corresponding angles (equal, same side of transversal), alternate angles (equal, between the lines on opposite sides), and co-interior angles (supplementary, adding to 180°). Vertically opposite angles are also equal. These relationships are used to find unknown angles in geometric problems.

sections.heading: Properties of Parallelograms and Rhombuses
sections.content: A parallelogram has: opposite sides parallel and equal; opposite angles equal; diagonals that bisect each other. A rhombus has all properties of a parallelogram plus: all four sides equal; diagonals bisect each other at right angles; diagonals bisect the vertex angles. A rectangle has all parallelogram properties plus all angles are 90°. A square has all rhombus properties plus all angles are 90°.

sections.heading: The Intercept Theorem
sections.content: If three or more parallel lines cut equal intercepts on one transversal, they cut equal intercepts on any other transversal. Proof uses congruent triangles (AAS condition): given parallel lines through A, B, C cutting another transversal at X, Y, Z with AB = BC, then XY = YZ.

sections.heading: Congruent Triangles
sections.content: Two triangles are congruent if they have exactly the same shape and size. The four conditions for congruency are: SAS (two sides and included angle equal); ASA or AAS (two angles and corresponding side equal); SSS (three sides equal); RHS (right angle, hypotenuse, and one other side equal). Congruent triangles are used to prove properties of geometric figures.
